title: Lovable in 15 Minutes: Build an App, Pitch Deck AND Marketing (No Code)

segments.text: This guy has no tech background, never
learned to code, runs a business making
over a hundred thousand dollars a month
building software for clients. The skill
that made it possible, he just describes
what he wants in plain English and a
tool builds it for him. The tool is
called Lovable. I run three companies
and I consult [music] founders. Whenever
something is being done manually, we
don't hire for it, we build a software
solution. Last [music] month we launched
four internal products. But here's the
thing, building a product today is
easier than it's ever been. The hard
part hasn't changed. Getting it into
people's hands, getting [music] them to
actually pay you, that's a completely
different game. You need the product,
the pitch deck, the marketing, the whole
[music] launch and today I'm going to do
all of it from one tool. Build a working
app with AI inside it, generate a
PowerPoint pitch deck, create a product
PDF, write LinkedIn launch post and this
tool handles all of it and I'm showing
you the whole thing.
One more thing, every prompt I use is in
the free WhatsApp group. You can find
the link in the description. [music]
All right, so here's what we're doing.
Imagine you're running a small business
or you're freelancing. You've got
clients, you've got deals happening and
you want to manage all of it on one
single dashboard. You don't want to buy
some expensive subscription to
Salesforce or HubSpot. You basically
want to build what big companies use,
but the small version that's
specifically made for your use case.
That's what we're building today, a
client tracker. [music]
Whatever you want to build booking
system, inventory tracker, project
dashboard, same process. I'm using this
because it touches every feature
available in Lovable. So let's go to
lovable.com. When you are here, this is
how Lovable looks. You can log in or you
can just start prompting directly. Let's

management app. I need a contacts page
with name, company, email and status. A
deals pipeline where I can drag deals
between stages, [music] new lead,
proposal sent, negotiating, did I win
the sale, did I lose the sale. I also
want a dashboard which shows total
deals, conversion rate, total revenue
and the design should be clean, [music]
modern with sidebar navigation. Now,
before it starts building, I'll turn on
plan mode. What this does is it tells
Lovable to plan everything first, all
the pages we need, all the data we need,
all the design. It plans [music]
everything and shows me the whole thing
before it goes and writes the code and
builds the software. So it's looking at
the features, the UI structure, it's
figured out we need a contacts page, a
deals pipeline, a dashboard, sidebar
navigation. If you're not technical,
don't worry about the specific names
here. If you are technical, you can

wanted to change anything, add a page,
remove a feature, change the layout, I
would tell it right now before it starts
building. That's the whole point of plan
mode. You get to review and adjust
before any code gets written. But this
looks solid, so I'll approve the plan
and now it's working like an actual
developer. It's figured out on its own
that it needs a design system, a
dashboard page, a contacts [music] page
and a deals pipeline and it's building
all of them at the same time. And there

the basic contacts page [music] and the
deals pipeline, all working from one
prompt. But if you look at this, it's
looking pretty plain. I want to give it
a different feel, I want to make it look
like something someone would actually
want to use, actually pay money to use.
And we want to build software that you
can sell to other people. So let's fix
the design. So before designing, you
need to have some design clarity in your
mind. What kind of design do you want?
And for that, you have to look over the

>> [music]
>> Find the kind of design style you want

because right now, if I close this

I know, so many jargon words. Let me
explain it simply. A database is
basically a place where all the
information gets saved. If a user comes
and fills in your form, fills in their
details in your client tracker, all of

>> [music]

That's a domain. You can connect an

If you want a developer to pick it up

You don't have to do anything manually.

Let me show you. Next, [music] a
